channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: If AC Becomes Zero 100 Times a Second, Why Doesn’t the Light Turn Off?
item.description: Everybody knows that AC in India operates at 50 Hz. And most of us also know that Alternating Current follows a sinusoidal waveform. It rises, falls, becomes zero, reverses direction, and repeats...

item.title: Stop Shipping ML Models With Bare Floats: A Deep Dive Into Statistically Rigorous Model Evaluation
item.description: Stop Shipping ML Models With Bare Floats Every week, somewhere, a team makes a deployment decision that looks like this: Model A: AUROC = 0.847 Model B: AUROC = 0.851 They ship Model B. Maybe it's...

item.title: Why I Stopped Pretending to Know Everything (and Started Asking "Dumb" Questions)
item.description: Not long ago, I was in a meeting where someone said, "We'll just validate that through the API layer." I nodded along, as if I knew exactly what that meant. I didn't. For the next twenty minutes,...

item.title: # 👽 Verifying an Alien Dictionary (LeetCode 953)
item.description: Imagine you're interviewing on another planet. 🌎➡️🛸 The aliens use the same English lowercase letters (a-z), but their alphabet order is completely different! Your task is to determine whether a...

item.title: Optional persistent data example:
item.description: If you’re still generating unit files with podman generate systemd, there’s a better path now: Quadlet. In current Podman docs, podman generate systemd is marked deprecated (still available, but...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two malicious cyber campaigns that exhibit similarities with a persistent North Korean threat cluster known as Contagious Interview (aka Famous Chollima,...

item.title: We were losing leads every night. Here is what we built to fix it.
item.description: For the first 6 months of building chatbot.witarist.com, we did something a lot of early-stage founders do: we left the website chat offline at night. We thought it was fine. Most of our customers...

item.title: Accelerating researchers and developers building multilingual AI with a new open dataset
item.description: A new repository-level dataset, published on GitHub under CC0-1.0, helps researchers and developers discover multilingual developer content across READMEs, issues, and pull requests. The post...

item.title: How I Assess Engineering Health in My First Week at a New Org
item.description: The diagnostic framework I run every time I join a new engineering organization, and why the data is only half the picture. The first week at a new company is a lot. You're meeting strangers,...

item.title: PCM Audio Explained: How Sound Becomes Numbers
item.description: Whenever you listen to a WAV file, record audio through a microphone, or process speech data for machine learning, you are likely working with PCM audio. But what exactly is PCM, and why is it so...

item.title: The Thread Battle: Go Concurrency vs. Node.js Event Loop from First Principles
item.description: When building high-concurrency backend services, two ecosystems dominate the conversation: Node.js and Go (Golang). If you ask the internet how they handle concurrency, you’ll get the standard...

item.title: All the news about Anthropic’s new AI fight with the White House
item.description: Anthropic was already navigating one dispute with the government in its standoff with the Pentagon, and then came an order on June 12th to block off foreign access to its most recently released AI...

item.title: Why do South Koreans love AI so much?
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. When I landed in Seoul after a grueling 12-hour flight...

item.title: The best early Amazon Prime Day deals so far
item.description: Amazon’s earlier-than-usual Prime Day doesn’t begin until June 23rd, but there are several even earlier deals on must-have products that you can check out right now. To name some examples, Apple’s...

item.title: Meta’s new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
item.description: Meta announced Monday that it's rolling out a wave of new AI features on Facebook, the latest sign of the company's effort to catch up in the AI race and keep users more engaged on the platform.

item.title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
item.description: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and...

item.title: Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
item.description: At Washington's request, Anthropic suddenly took its newest and most powerful AI models offline over the weekend. The American company said it had little choice after the White House demanded it...

item.title: Google Chrome is closing the loopholes that let old ad blockers keep working
item.description: Google Chrome version 150 and 151, expected in late June and July, respectively, will cut off support for the last remaining workarounds for running older ad blockers, 9to5Google reports. Google...

item.title: Users cry foul after AMD stripped memory crypto from its consumer CPUs
item.description: AMD's stripping of TSME from consumer CPUs appears to be a deliberate, covert move.

item.title: Big Tech’s desperate last push at AI regulation
item.description: For months, Big Tech's Washington lobbyists have chased after the holy grail of pro-AI legislation: preemption. This would be a comprehensive federal law, passed in Congress and signed by the...

item.title: Xbox turmoil continues with a studio closure and executive departures
item.description: Last week, Xbox boss Asha Sharma sent a memo warning of an Xbox "reset" ahead of expected layoffs, and today, Kotaku reported that Xbox plans to shut down Compulsion Games, the studio behind South...

item.title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Lets Low-Privilege Users Take Over AI Gateway Servers
item.description: A default low-privilege account on a LiteLLM proxy can climb to full admin and run code on the server by chaining three vulnerabilities, researchers at Obsidian Security disclosed LiteLLM is a...

item.title: Roblox exec says ticking a box for age verification is ‘not enough anymore’
item.description: Roblox's vice president of safety product policy, Eliza Jacobs, told NBC News that Roblox is "optimistic" that its new facial age estimation tech will "continue to get better," saying, "Ticking a...

item.title: Cybersecurity vets protest ‘dangerous’ US government ban on Anthropic’s most powerful models
item.description: A group made up of dozens of cybersecurity experts urged the White House to remove export control restrictions on Anthropic’s models Fable and Mythos, arguing that the order is going to limit the...

item.title: This man with ALS is “the first power user” of a brain implant that lets him speak
item.description: Casey Harrell has had a set of electrodes embedded in his brain for almost three years. Harrell, who has amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) and is paralyzed, first used his brain-computer...

item.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
item.description: A single click on a trusted Microsoft link could have let an attacker pull emails, calendar details, and indexed files out of Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search. Researchers at Varonis Threat...

item.title: We’re strengthening our presence in Alabama through new investments and community support.
item.description: Google has announced a $1.5 billion investment for 2026 and 2027 to expand its data center campus in Jackson County, Alabama. Operating since 2019 on a repurposed former…

item.title: My favorite Qi2 power bank is cheaper than ever for Verge readers
item.description: I recently published a buying guide on the best Qi2 power banks. Out of seven feature-packed batteries, I really dug the Baseus AM52 10,000mAh power bank that has a built-in USB-C cable. It’s fast...

item.title: Salesforce acquires AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6 billion
item.description: Salesforce says it wants to use Fin's team and technology to improve Agentforce, its existing enterprise platform that businesses can use to build custom AI agents that automate tasks.

item.title: The Verge’s Father’s Day 2026 gift guide
item.description: With Father's Day on the horizon, happening June 21st, it's time to start thinking about what kind of gifts you want to buy for the fathers you care about. You know your dad best - he may be a man...
